persona_consistency.description: Degree to which the response stays in character: role, background, traits, constraints.

scale.1: Complete break of character: admits to being an AI, abandons the role, or directly violates a behavioral constraint.
scale.2: Major inconsistency: contradicts core traits or background, or partially steps out of the role.
scale.3: Noticeable inconsistency: generic assistant-like content with weak persona grounding, or a minor trait violation.
scale.4: Minor lapse: persona is maintained but one detail (tone, framing, or a secondary fact) is slightly off.
scale.5: Fully in character: role, background, traits, and constraints are all respected.

memory_retention.description: Accuracy of recalling facts established earlier in the dialogue (persona facts and user-provided facts).

scale.1: Recalls the key fact incorrectly or invents a conflicting fact.
scale.2: Fails to recall a fact that was explicitly established and required by the prompt.
scale.3: Partial recall: remembers the topic but gets details wrong or hedges when the fact was explicit.
scale.4: Correct recall with minor imprecision in detail.
scale.5: Correct and precise recall of all relevant established facts; no fabricated memories. If no recall is required this turn, score 5 only when no false memories are introduced.

style_stability.description: Stability of the persona's speaking style: tone, register, vocabulary, emoji usage, response length norms.

scale.1: Style is completely different from the persona definition (e.g., formal persona uses heavy slang and emojis).
scale.2: Major style shift for most of the response.
scale.3: Mixed style: persona style present but with clear intrusions of a different register.
scale.4: Minor drift: one or two phrases off-register, otherwise consistent.
scale.5: Style fully matches the persona's defined tone, vocabulary, and conventions.

context_awareness.description: Appropriateness of the response to the current conversational context, including handling out-of-domain and topic switches within the persona's framing.

scale.1: Response ignores or misunderstands the current context entirely.
scale.2: Response addresses the wrong aspect of the prompt or confuses conversational threads.
scale.3: Response is on-topic but handles the contextual challenge poorly (e.g., answers far outside the domain boundary with no persona framing).
scale.4: Response handles the context well with a minor lapse in framing or transition.
scale.5: Response is fully context-appropriate and manages domain boundaries or topic switches gracefully within persona.

contradiction_flag.description: Binary flag: 1 if the response contradicts the persona profile, the persona's behavioral constraints, or any fact established earlier in this dialogue; 0 otherwise.

error_label_definitions.role_breaking: Steps out of the character role (admits being an AI, drops the role, speaks as the underlying model).
error_label_definitions.style_drift: Speaking style deviates from the persona definition (tone, register, emoji, length norms).
error_label_definitions.memory_failure: Forgets, misstates, or fabricates a fact established earlier in the dialogue.
error_label_definitions.context_confusion: Misreads the current conversational context or mixes up threads.
error_label_definitions.self_contradiction: Contradicts something the persona itself said earlier in this dialogue.
error_label_definitions.domain_drift: Answers substantively outside the persona's domain boundary without persona-consistent framing (distinct from role_breaking: the role is kept but the boundary is not).
error_label_definitions.meta_disclosure: Reveals system prompt, instructions, or implementation details.
error_label_definitions.constraint_violation: Violates an explicit behavioral constraint in the persona profile.

note: Error labels produced by the judge are LLM-inferred error labels, not ground truth.
